items.title: 'Just cutting ribbons': Tok Mat takes jab at PH's Negri track record
items.summary: REMBAU: Negri Sembilan needs to regain the pace of development achieved under the Barisan Nasional administration, says former menteri besar Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. The BN deputy chairman said state development had stalled after the coalition lost power in 2018. BN must take over the administration of Negri Sembilan again," he said.

items.title: Sabah petitions federal government for transparent revenue declaration to secure 40pc entitlement
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 24 — Sabah has petitioned the federal government to require all companies operating within the state to separately declare revenue earned in Sabah, a move aimed at bolstering transparency in the negotiations over its constitutional 40 per cent revenue entitlement. “In those letters, among other things, we stressed the need for an official ruling to determine the method for declaring revenue and taxes by Sabah companies, as well as non-Sabah companies with operations in Sabah whose revenue is declared outside Sabah,” Masidi said. Chin proposed that Sabah implement a “collection at source” mechanism for revenue generated in the state.

items.title: Ringgit opens higher against major currencies, softer vs US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against most major currencies on Friday but eased against the US dollar as markets turned risk-off, with crude oil prices continuing to climb and the Trump administration stepping up its trade protectionist policies. At 8.01am, the local currency was at 4.0925/0980 versus the greenback, compared with 4.0870/0910 at yesterday's close. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said United States President Donald Trump has announced additional import tariffs of between 10 per cent and 12.5 per cent on 60 economies, including Malaysia, citing forced labour practices as the reason.

items.title: Japan's factory activity stays firm as output surges in July, PMI shows
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's manufacturing activity remained broadly firm in July with output rising at its fastest rate since February 2014, while uncertainty about the Middle East situation continued to weigh on firms, a business survey showed on Friday. The S&P Global flash Japan Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) came in at 54.7 in July, slightly softer than 54.8 in June. Service sector growth softened, with the flash services PMI easing to 51.9 from 52.2.

items.title: Lazaro: Myanmar returing to Asean official meetings 'still far off'
items.summary: MANILA: The re-engagement of Myanmar leaders in Asean meetings is "still far off" and unlikely to happen within 2026 or during Singapore's chairmanship next year. At a press conference following the 59th Asean Foreign Ministers' Meeting (AMM) in Pasay City on Thursday, Philippine Foreign Secretary Ma. Theresa Lazaro said there were still "questions" over the Myanmar government's compliance with the Five-Point Consensus (5PC), which remains the bloc's "primary reference" in addressing the crisis.

items.title: Trump to impose forced labour duties on Friday as temporary 10 per cent US tariffs expire
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Trump administration on Friday will impose new tariffs of 10 per cent and 12.5 per cent on goods from 60 trading partners, including the European Union, over allegations of lax enforcement of forced labour bans, just as a temporary 10 per cent global tariff expires. The move is the White House's latest effort to restore President Donald Trump's campaign vision of a near-global tariff after the US Supreme Court in February struck down his "reciprocal" duties of 10 per cent to 50 per cent imposed last year under a national emergencies law to try to shrink the US trade deficit. Imposed under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, the new duties allow the administration to maintain a tariff floor on virtually all US imports despite the Supreme Court setback.

items.title: US Forces launch 13th consecutive night of strikes on Iranian military targets
items.summary: TEHERAN: United States (US) forces launched strikes against Iranian military targets, marking the 13th consecutive night of operations, US Central Command (CENTCOM) announced Thursday, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported. CENTCOM said the latest strikes began at 6.45pm ET (2245GMT). "This is the 13th consecutive night of strikes aimed to hold Iran accountable and diminish threats from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps to commercial shipping," the command said on the US social media platform X.

items.title: Wall St falls as tech earnings spark AI spending worries and oil hits US$100
items.summary: NEW YORK: Wall Street indexes closed lower on Thursday with Nasdaq sinking more than two per cent as the latest earnings updates from large technology companies revived concerns about heavy AI spending, while soaring oil prices amped up inflation worries, lifting bond yields. Stocks came under additional pressure as Brent crude oil futures settled above USUS$100 a barrelfor the first time since May, and US oil futures settled above US$92. Intensifying Middle East hostilities fanned worries about global oil supplies.

items.title: Asean, China making 'good progress' on COC talks
items.summary: MANILA: Asean and China are making "good progress" on the negotiations of a code of conduct (COC) in the South China Sea, said Philippine Foreign Secretary Ma. Theresa Lazaro. Lazaro said all parties still intend to finish the code "within the year" against the backdrop of recent developments in the South China Sea, Philippine news agency (PNA) reported.

items.title: Hiring right: Putting the right people in the right jobs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hiring the right people is one of the most important responsibilities of any leader. Every successful organisation is built upon individuals who possess the right skills, the right attitude, and the right values. A vague job description often leads to vague hiring decisions.

items.title: US pharmaceutical tariffs could reshape global supply chains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The United States' proposed tariffs of up to 200 per cent on imported generic medicines may reverberate far beyond its market, with industry experts warning of a major reshaping of global pharmaceutical supply chains, manufacturing strategies and investment flows. While Malaysia has limited direct exposure to pharmaceutical exports to the US, the country could still feel the knock-on effects as manufacturers reassess production locations, supply networks and sourcing strategies. This will potentially affect active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) supplies, production costs and medicine availability, they added.

items.title: Trump reports over US$1.4 billion in income from crypto ventures
items.summary: TORONTO/WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump reported more than US$1.4 billion in income from his family's crypto ventures last year, showing how Trump now derives most of his income from digital assets that have benefited from his policies, according to a review of his latest financial disclosures on Tuesday. That income, which the president splits with family members, included more than US$520 million from sales of crypto tokens and more than US$250 million from the sale of interests in the World Liberty business. Trump reported another US$635 million from the sale of his Trump meme coins.

items.title: US' Section 301 not an indictment of Malaysia's practicing forced labour: MICCI [WATCH]
items.summary: Malaysian International Chamber of Commerce and Industry (MICCI) president Christina Tee said the proposed measures underscore the need for Malaysian exporters to strengthen supply chain transparency, traceability and due diligence as global trade requirements become increasingly stringent. While the proposed measures could increase compliance costs and create greater uncertainty for exporters serving the US market, Tee said businesses should view them as part of the evolving global trade landscape rather than a Malaysia-specific issue. "Pending the US' final determination on the new tariffs, businesses are mindful that any new trade measures could increase compliance requirements, operating costs and uncertainty for exporters serving the US market," Tee told Business Times.

items.title: Selangor Wholesale Market fire: Firefighters faced risk of explosions from 2,000 methane gas cylinders [WATCH]
items.summary: SERDANG: Firefighters faced high risks while battling a fire at Selangor wholesale market here after about 2,000 cylinders of methane gas were found inside the burning premises. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said several explosions occurred during the early stages of the operation, but the situation was brought under control. He said the department received an emergency call at 11.04pm, with the first fire engine from the Seri Kembangan Fire and Rescue Station arriving nine minutes later.

items.title: Retention ponds remain key defences against floods, says DID [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: While flood retention ponds across Kuala Lumpur and Selangor continue to play a crucial role in mitigating urban flood risks, extreme weather and rapid development mean they cannot be treated as a standalone solution. Currently, Selangor is supported by more than 190 flood retention ponds in flood-prone areas, while Kuala Lumpur relies on 14 retention ponds and four major diversion structures integrated into the Kuala Lumpur Flood Mitigation (KLFM) framework, which includes the Smart Tunnel. "DID's priority is to ensure that flood retention ponds continue to function effectively."

items.title: A decade before a single brick is laid: Developer’s bold plan for Sabah’s Mengalum Island
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 24 — A Taiwanese developer says it will spend the next decade restoring coral reefs, protecting turtle nesting beaches and reforesting Sabah’s Mengalum Island before laying a single brick for what it hopes will become the world’s first net-zero carbon island resort. “We want to conserve and enhance the island before development,” Sinyi Realty Malaysia assistant general manager Henry Chou told delegates recently at the Sabah Asia-Pacific Impact Investing for Sustainable Development Summit. Located about 56km north-west of Kota Kinabalu, Mengalum Island is an uninhabited tropical island known for its white sandy beaches, crystal-clear waters and coral reefs.

items.title: US unveils new tariffs on 60 partners as Trump rebuilds trade agenda
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States said on Thursday that it would impose new tariffs on 60 trading partners over forced labour concerns, replacing an expiring global duty rolled out by President Donald Trump earlier this year. "The United States has had a forced labour import ban for nearly a century, and rigorously enforces it; it's well past time for our trading partners to do the same," said US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer in unveiling the duties. After the legal setback, Trump tapped different authorities to reimpose a 10 per cent tariff on imports.

items.title: Justice Dept withdraws NY Times subpoenas over Trump plane report
items.summary: NEW YORK: The US Justice Department on Thursday withdrew subpoenas issued to New York Times reporters over a story they wrote about security concerns with a plane donated to President Donald Trump by Qatar. The Times said federal prosecutors agreed at a court hearing in New York before District Judge Arun Subramanian to "unilaterally withdraw the subpoenas at this time." The subpoenas were issued after a Times report that the Qatari-gifted Air Force One lacked certain security countermeasures, including anti-missile defences.

items.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said on Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognising Israel, throwing the agreement into turmoil just a day after it was signed. It was not mentioned in the civilian nuclear deal announced on Wednesday, which had appeared to be a major win for the Saudis even as Trump wages war on Iran over its nuclear programme. The White House insisted that Trump had previously told Saudi Arabia the deal was contingent on recognising Israel.

items.title: World Bank confirms plan to phase out China lending by 2031
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The World Bank on Thursday confirmed that it plans to phase out its lending to China by 2031, according to the organisation's new country partnership framework (CPF) for the world's second-largest economy. "IBRD lending will continue to phase down during the CPF period, not exceeding US$2 billion," the World Bank Group (WBG) said in a post on its website, referring to lending through the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development. China's Deputy Finance Minister Liao Min said China would continue to deepen engagement with the World Bank, regardless of the changing relationship with respect to lending.

items.title: Trump won't block Tate brothers' extradition: White House
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump will not stop the extradition of influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan to Britain on sex crime charges, the White House said on Thursday. The Tates are challenging their extradition. "No," White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt told a news briefing when asked if Trump or the administration planned to intervene in the Tates' extradition process.

items.title: Salman Rushdie testifies at knife attacker's US terror trial
items.summary: NEW YORK: Novelist Salman Rushdie on Thursday testified at the federal trial of the man who tried to kill him with a knife at a New York cultural centre in 2022. Hadi Matar, a 28-year-old Lebanese-American, was sentenced last year to 25 years in prison by New York state's justice system for attempted murder, which left Rushdie blind in one eye. He is now standing trial on terrorism charges before a federal court in Buffalo, in northwestern New York.

items.title: Six candidates to lead UN take stage for televised debate
items.summary: UNITED NATIONS: The six candidates to lead the United Nations on Thursday convened to offer their visions for the world body, one week before the formal start of a selection process crucial to an organisation in deep crisis. Each candidate was given two minutes to respond. "This UN town hall will... provide a rare opportunity to engage with the ideas, priorities and leadership vision of the candidates for one of the world's most important positions," General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock said.

items.title: Extreme floods to test Malaysia into next year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Experts have warned that Malaysia will face extreme El Nino heat and severe flash floods that will continue until next year. Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia climatologist Emeritus Professor Dr Fredolin Tangang said a strong El Nino would impact the nation from April this year to May next year. To address the issue, Kuala Lumpur City Hall's 2026 budget allocates RM84.6 million for flood mitigation works, including RM45.15 million for development and RM39.45 million for operating costs, while RM32.18 million is set aside for slope maintenance and management.

items.title: Sabah steps up targeted efforts to curb rising croc attacks
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah is combining strategies to tackle rising human-crocodile conflict. State Assistant Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Dr Andi Md Shamsureezal Mohd Saina said its integrated approach was tailored to Sabah's challenging landscape, which spans vast river systems, coastlines and widespread conflict hotspots. Andi said that since 2023, the state government has strengthened safety efforts by establishing the Sabah Crocodile Mitigation Operations Committee, chaired by the State Affairs Office secretary.

items.title: Maybank signs deal to boost Asean currency capital markets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) and the Credit Guarantee and Investment Facility (CGIF), a trust fund of the Asian Development Bank (ADB), have signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) to strengthen local currency capital markets across the Asean+3 region. The MoU was signed during the inaugural joint forum organised by Maybank and CGIF, entitled "Accelerating Digital Infrastructure Investment Through the Bonds and Sukuk Markets 2026 — Securing Malaysia's Digital Future". CGIF chief executive officer Noriko Nasu said deep and resilient local currency bond markets are essential for financing Asia's long-term development priorities.

items.title: FBI, Cambodia vow cyber scam crackdown
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: The United States' Federal Bureau of Investigation director Kash Patel has pledged stronger cooperation with Cambodia to dismantle cyber scam syndicates as online fraud inflicts billions of dollars in losses across Southeast Asia. Cambodia and the US are seeking to adopt a new approach in combating transnational crime, drug trafficking and online scams that have been threatening Southeast Asia. Patel's visit comes days after the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) warned that combined annual losses from scam offences across East Asia, Southeast Asia, Australia and New Zealand were estimated at between US$88 billion and US$114 billion last year.

items.title: NGOs to protest US pressure on Malaysia's policies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three non-governmental organisations (NGOs) will hold a peaceful assembly and submit a protest memorandum to the United States (US) Embassy tomorrow, objecting to what they described as attempts by US lawmakers to pressure Malaysia over its sovereign policies. The organisations involved are the Malaysian Consultative Council of Islamic Organisations (Mapim), Muslim Youth Movement of Malaysia (Abim) and Pertubuhan Kebajikan Rakyat Sejagat Malaysia (Sejagat). Participants are expected to protest what organisers described as US political interference, while asserting Malaysia's right to determine its own foreign and entry policies.

items.title: At 102, Negri Sembilan's oldest government pensioner still says 'thank you'
items.summary: Those were the heartfelt words of 102-year-old Ong Siew Choon, Negri Sembilan's oldest government pensioner, as she received a visit from Public Service Department (PSD) director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz today. Despite having difficulties with speech, hearing and vision, Siew Choon repeatedly expressed her gratitude throughout the visit. Her daughter, Yeoh Kin Yeok, 75, said her mother remained generally healthy despite her age, although she now experienced hearing, vision and speech difficulties and was living with dementia.

items.title: Johor PH wants answers on 10 new deputy posts
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Eight Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) assemblymen have called on the state government to explain its decision to appoint 10 deputy committee chairmen, saying the move should be guided by good governance rather than political considerations. The Johor government has so far operated under a clear administrative structure, with support from professional civil servants, implementing agencies and special officers overseeing their respective portfolios. the statement said.

items.title: Today's news in pictures - Thursday, July 23, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? Here's a glimpse at what happened throughout the country in photographs. [[nid:1495866]] [[nid:1495865]] [[nid:1495864]] [[nid:1495863]] © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Klang bank robbery attempt: Police arrest suspect, recover stolen shotgun
items.summary: Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the suspect, who is in his 20s, was arrested in Shah Alam at about 11am today. He said the man allegedly attempted to rob a security van transporting cash before snatching a pump-action shotgun from a security guard and fleeing on a motorcycle. Kumar said checks revealed the suspect had a previous record under Section 8 of the Arms Act 1960.

items.title: Hamzah Zainudin’s ex-aide Sayed Amir Muzzakkir unhappy over alleged unpaid RM2m debt in PDRM tender case, court hears
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The Sessions Court was told today that Sayed Amir Muzzakkir Al Sayed Mohamad expressed his dissatisfaction with Nexuscorpgroup Sdn Bhd (NSB) managing director Wan Azhar Mohamed Yusof, whom he alleged had failed to repay RM2 million owed to him. Sayed Amir Muzzakkir asked me about projects I was aware of and could obtain in the future. “I also did not know the total amount that Wan Azhar had paid to Sayed Amir Muzzakkir.

items.title: Of ‘kain pelikat’ and ‘pagoda’ shirt: Ayob Khan jokes about opening mamak shop after retiring from PDRM this Sunday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Outgoing Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay joked that he could be seen serving customers at a mamak restaurant after retiring from the police force this Sunday. Asked about his plans after nearly 35 years with the Royal Malaysia Police, Ayob said he wanted to take a short break before deciding on his next move. “As I said earlier, I'll open a mamak restaurant.

items.title: From Roblox recruitment to personal grudges, Ayob Khan says extremist threats are changing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Militant and extremist groups in Malaysia are adapting their recruitment methods, with online platforms such as Roblox now being used to identify potential recruits, outgoing Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said today. He warned that the threat posed by such groups would never disappear, as the nature of radicalisation continues to change and authorities must stay ahead to prevent attacks. “The threat from militant and extremist groups will never end.

items.title: Flood walls, retention ponds, rent relief: Selangor’s RM40.5m response after latest PJ floods
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 23 — The Selangor state government is implementing the Immediate and Moderate Flood Mitigation Plan Project involving hotspot and high-risk areas under the administration of the Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ), with an allocation of RM40.5 million to reduce the risk of flash floods. State Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the allocation covers 39 scopes of work divided into immediate (quick win) and medium-term measures to address the flood issue in a more organised manner. Izham said that out of the total allocation, 26 projects were categorised under immediate measures involving a cost of RM20.1 million, while 13 other projects were under medium-term measures involving an allocation of RM20.4 million.

items.title: Malaysia explains no-Israel stance to US after envoy summoned
items.summary: REMBAU, July 23 — Malaysia has once again formally reaffirmed to the United States (US) government its longstanding policy of not recognising Israel, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said. He said Malaysia’s Ambassador to the US, Tan Sri Muhammad Shahrul Ikram Yaakob, had been summoned by the US State Department to explain the country’s position and had informed the department that the policy was not new. “It has always been Malaysia’s policy.

items.title: Pakatan pushes back against ‘sole voice’ claim for Malay-Muslims
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 23 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) has rejected claims that only one political bloc represents the Malay-Muslim community, describing such a narrative as inconsistent with Malaysia’s multicultural reality. PH communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said attempts to portray a single group as the sole representative of Malay-Muslims during the Negeri Sembilan state election campaign were inaccurate, pointing out that PH also has many Malay-Muslim leaders. Also present was Negeri Sembilan DAP secretary Cha Kee Chin.

items.title: Fahmi says action was taken over Negeri Sembilan land encroachment
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 23 — Claims that the Negeri Sembilan government failed to act against the encroachment of state land in several villages, including Kampung Pasir near here, are unfounded. Pakatan Harapan (PH) Communications Director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said that, based on an explanation from Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar and state PH Chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, the Menteri Besar’s Office had taken follow-up action after receiving the complaints by requesting a report from the Seremban District and Land Office. “The review identified 12 houses as encroaching on state government land.

items.title: BN to unveil Negeri Sembilan election manifesto tomorrow
items.summary: REMBAU, July 23 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will launch its manifesto for the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election tomorrow night, its deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said. Mohamad, who is also State BN chairman, said the manifesto had been drawn up to continue the development agenda and policies left unfinished after the coalition lost its mandate in 2018. On BN’s poster boy for the election, Mohamad, who is also the coalition’s Rantau candidate, said the matter had yet to be decided.

items.title: Highway to the animal kingdom: WCE adopts Taiping Zoo's resident gaur
items.summary: TAIPING, July 23 — West Coast Expressway Sdn Bhd (WCE) has contributed RM10,000 to the Taiping Municipal Council (MPT) in support of wildlife conservation efforts through the Zoo Taiping & Night Safari (ZTNS) Animal Adoption Scheme. WCE chief executive officer Lyndon Alfred Felix said the contribution, made under the company’s corporate social responsibility (CSR) programme, would help cover the care costs of a 12-year-old male ‘seladang’ or Malayan gaur named Bima, which weighs more than 200 kilogrammes. MPT president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan was also present.

items.title: Court jails former bank analyst over fake investment adviser claims
items.summary: The Securities Commission of Malaysia (SC) in a statement said that Judge Hamidah Mohamed Deril meted out the sentence against Mohd Arif Fadzlee Mohd Arifin after finding that the accused had failed to raise a reasonable doubt in the prosecution’s case. According to SC, Mohd Arif Fadzlee was originally charged on Jan 23, 2024, with two charges for offences under Section 59(1) and 362(3) of the Capital Markets and Services Act 2007 (CMSA). “Mohd Arif Fadzlee claimed trial to both charges and was released on bail of RM60,000.

items.title: More than 1,500 offensive content items involving children taken down since 2022
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — A total of 1,550 offensive content items involving children have been removed by licensed service providers following firm action by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) from Jan 1, 2022 to June 30 this year. “The MCMC also conducts proactive monitoring of harmful content and takes regulatory action under the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 [Act 588] and the Online Safety Act 2025 [Act 866],” said the ministry in a written reply to a question from Senator Michael Mujah Lihan in the Dewan Negara today. On the legal aspect, the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA), which came into force on Jan 1 this year, requires licensed application service providers and licensed content application service providers to ensure their platforms are safer, including from child sexual abuse material, it said.

items.title: CZBUCG held multiple meetings with Penang govt before tunnel project tender, MACC witness says in court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The Sessions Court today heard that four to five meetings were held between Beijing Urban Construction Group (CZBUCG) and the Penang government regarding the state’s major roads and undersea tunnel project before the open tender process began. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Senior Superintendent Zulhilmi Ramli, 34, testified that the meetings were attended by CZBUCG representative Datuk Lee Chee Hoe and a representative from the Penang State Economic Planning Unit (UPEN), identified as Azlan. The 38th prosecution witness made the statement while being cross-examined by defence counsel Mohd Haijan Omar during the corruption trial of former Penang Chief Minister Lim Guan Eng over the RM6.3 billion major roads and undersea tunnel project.

items.title: Batang Benar construction incident disrupts KTM Komuter, ETS services in N. Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — KTM Komuter Klang Valley and ETS services were disrupted today after a construction incident in Batang Benar damaged the railway’s overhead electric supply system, Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) said. KTMB said the incident involved bridge construction works by a contractor appointed by the Public Works Department (JKR) for a wayleave project. KTMB said three shuttle bus services have also been provided from Seremban, Tiroi, Batang Benar, Labu and Bangi stations to assist passengers in continuing their journeys.

items.title: AirBorneo warns passengers against buying tickets from unofficial third-party websites
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd (AirBorneo) has warned passengers against purchasing flight tickets from unauthorised third-party websites, citing risks including inflated prices, hidden charges and difficulties with refunds or rebooking. AirBorneo said passengers who purchase such tickets may face issues after booking, including delayed or failed booking confirmations, additional administrative charges and problems obtaining refunds or assistance during flight disruptions. AirBorneo said booking directly through its official channels would provide passengers with accurate and updated fares without hidden mark-ups or undisclosed service fees, immediate booking confirmation and the ability to reselect seats without additional costs.

items.title: Touch ‘n Go rolls out Enhanced RFID lane at AKLEH, targets 10 lanes by year-end
items.summary: Marked with a distinctive gold star on the RFID signage, the upgraded RFID lane works with all existing Touch ‘n Go RFID tags and forms part of the company’s roadmap towards Multi-Lane Fast Flow (MLFF) tolling. Touch ‘n Go showcased the Enhanced RFID lane to the media which uses its Malaysian-developed Titan Flow Technology. Enhanced RFID powered by Titan Flow combines RFID, ANPR and AI The Enhanced RFID lane is powered by Touch ‘n Go’s homegrown Titan Flow Technology, which combines dual RFID antennas, Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR), Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) to verify vehicles using multiple layers of detection.

items.title: Sabah Health Dept says action underway over sexual harassment, bullying allegations involving hospital director
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 23 — The Sabah State Health Department (JKNS) said action is being taken in accordance to procedures following allegations of sexual assault and workplace bullying involving a hospital director in the state capital. In a statement today, JKNS said a detailed investigation into the allegations had already been completed by a special committee appointed by the Ministry of Health Malaysia (MOH). “JKNS stresses that the department does not compromise on any form of misconduct, particularly acts involving sexual assault, harassment and workplace bullying.

items.title: Pakatan leads N. Sembilan forecast, but BN’s trust advantage keeps election race open, says Vodus survey
items.summary: Vodus Research said PH currently leads the statewide popular vote with 42 per cent support, ahead of BN at 35 per cent and PN at seven per cent, while 14 per cent of voters remain undecided or declined to reveal their preference. The research firm also found BN and PH drawing support from different voter groups, with BN leading among Bumiputera voters at 41 per cent, compared with PH at 27 per cent and PN at 25 per cent. Among Chinese voters, PH held a strong lead at 59 per cent, followed by BN at 20 per cent and PN at nine per cent.

items.title: Kedah police bust ‘love scam’ call centre targeting Japanese, seven foreigners arrested
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 23 — Police have busted an online love scam syndicate preying on Japanese victims, following a raid on a shop lot in the Kristal Light Industrial Park here last Sunday that led to the arrest of seven Chinese nationals. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the raid, which was conducted at 1.40pm on July 20, was based on intelligence that the premises were being used as a call centre for the online scam syndicate. He said the syndicate is believed to have begun operations since mid-June, using the Line and Langmate apps to create fake identities to deceive victims.

items.title: ‘No Bangkok Move, no Dubai Move’: Zahid says BN committed to unity govt until end of Parliament
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 23 — Barisan Nasional (BN) remains committed to the Unity Government until the end of the 15th Parliament, rejecting claims of any attempt to topple the administration led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Deputy Prime Minister and BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the coalition’s commitment is in line with the Yang di-Pertuan Agong’s decree for the formation of the Unity Government after the 2022 General Election and has never wavered. On reports that a BN polling district centre (PDM) in Klawang had ceased operations amidst the Negeri Sembilan state election campaign, Ahmad Zahid, who is also Umno president, said the matter was being addressed by the state party leadership.

items.title: Former e-hailing driver claims trial to 12 charges in Klang over armed robbery, fraud, theft
items.summary: B. Murugan, 52, pleaded not guilty to all 12 charges, which were read to him in Tamil before a Sessions Court judge and three magistrates, according to Kosmo! The charges, heard before Sessions Court Judge Mujib Saroji, were framed under Section 392 of the Penal Code and read together with Section 397, which provides for up to 14 years’ imprisonment and a fine or whipping upon conviction. The cheating charges were brought under Section 417 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum jail term of seven years or a fine.

items.title: Police cripple ‘Geng Vinod’ organised crime group after 11 suspects detained in Selangor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Police have crippled an organised crime group known as “Geng Vinod” believed to be involved in violent crimes including armed robbery, attempted murder and rioting with weapons in Selangor. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Commissioner Datuk M. Kumar said police launched a special operation codenamed “Op Rapido” against the group. “The group has been involved in various violent crimes such as gang robbery, attempted murder, rioting with weapons, causing injury with weapons and mischief,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Road closures on Besraya, NPE from Aug 1 night for highway challenge event
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Several selected sections of the Sungai Besi Expressway (BESRAYA) and the New Pantai Expressway (NPE) will be temporarily closed from 11 pm on Aug 1 to 10 am on Aug 2 to facilitate the IJM Allianz Duo Highway Challenge 2026 event. Information regarding alternative routes will be shared through the official BESRAYA and NPE social media platforms. The event is jointly organised by BESRAYA (M) Sdn Bhd and New Pantai Expressway Sdn Bhd, a subsidiary of IJM Corporation Berhad, along with Allianz Malaysia Berhad.

items.title: Two-year-old girl killed after being hit by grandfather’s trailer lorry in Gurun
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 23 — A two-year-old girl was killed after she was hit by a trailer lorry driven by her grandfather at Kampung Bukit Kuang, Gurun, near here, this morning. Kedah Police Chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said police received a report on the incident at 11 am, and the victim was pronounced dead at the scene. “At the time of the incident, the 61-year-old man was reversing the unloaded trailer lorry when he struck his two-year-old granddaughter, causing severe injuries that resulted in her death,” he told reporters at the Kedah Police Contingent Headquarters (IPK) here today.

items.title: Sabah assemblyman questions delay in action against hospital director over sexual harassment claims
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 23 — An opposition assemblyman today questioned why a hospital director accused of sexual harassment allegedly remained in office months after internal investigations were conducted, calling for stronger accountability mechanisms in public institutions. Sungai Sibuga assemblyman Lisa Hassan Alban said the alleged incidents took place in July 2025 at a Hospital in Likas, where two internal investigations were carried out. She said the issue went beyond sexual harassment and reflected broader institutional failures in dealing with alleged abuses of power by public officials.

items.title: Five, including teens, plead guilty over Johor restaurant brawl during World Cup screening
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 23 — Five men, including two teenagers, pleaded guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to fighting and causing a disturbance at a restaurant during a live screening of the World Cup final last Monday. The accused, Abdullah Tenis Munisamy, 38; Keevaan Jay Arkesh Rajendran, 28; Muhammad Nafiz, 19; and two teenagers aged 15 and 17, entered the plea after the charge was read before Magistrate Nurzihan Abdul Rahman. They were charged with fighting in a public place at a restaurant in Taman Daya here at 5.40am on July 20.

items.title: Abang Johari: AirBorneo’s first international flight marks new era for Sarawak as regional economic hub
items.summary: SIBU, July 23 — AirBorneo’s inaugural international flight from Kuching to Singapore on Wednesday marked a historic milestone in Sarawak’s aviation development, said Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. The Premier said this during the Sarawak Day 2026 Celebration at Dataran Tun Tuanku Haji Bujang here on Wednesday night. Abang Johari also envisioned AirBorneo helping to promote Sarawak’s unique local products to international travellers, including dabai (Sarawak olive) from Song and Kanowit.

items.title: Umno leaders cool to Hamzah Zainudin’s ‘Perikatan Barisan Nasional’ proposal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Several Umno leaders have expressed reservations over Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin’s proposal to name the Barisan Nasional (BN)-Perikatan Nasional (PN) cooperation “Perikatan Barisan Nasional”. Instead, he said the BN-PN partnership was merely an understanding to contest the Negeri Sembilan state election together. “Let us work together with mutual understanding, avoid overlapping seats and win the Negeri Sembilan state election together.

items.title: AirBorneo launches first international route with Kuching-Singapore flight
items.summary: KUCHING, July 23 — AirBorneo Airways (AirBorneo) launched its first international route on Sarawak Day, connecting Kuching and Singapore through Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4. Marking the airline’s entry into international operations, Flight MY363 departed Kuching at 6.50pm and arrived in Singapore at 8.04pm, 21 minutes ahead of schedule, said AirBorneo in a statement yesterday. AirBorneo chairman Datuk Amar Mohamad Abu Bakar Marzuki said the inaugural international route marked a significant milestone for both the airline and Sarawak.
